materialChanges: Expanded the existing NIST DLMF record to the exact definition, general-power, product, and quotient locations; added scoped exponential-parameterization and half-life claims and mapped them through learner-visible source notes without adding a source.
Narrowed objective-interpret-change to the threshold-time and half-life performances that the existing mastery items assess.

Corrected item-solve-exponential to accept the required three-decimal value 4.865 and removed descriptive grading prose from the other acceptableResponses arrays while retaining answer logic in their answers and rubrics.
Added prerequisite feedback, a misconception prediction and new-case recheck, and explanatory synthesis retrieval feedback in semantic check directives.
Separated all practice prompts from their later feedback and moved the independent transfer solution to the following synthesis scene.
Corrected ATTRIBUTION.md so it points to the committed review and adjudication artifacts instead of denying later review or finalization runs.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The existing DLMF record now covers Chapter 4 at a stable chapter URL and locates §4.2(i)-(ii) for definitions, inverse relations, and general powers plus §4.8(i) for product and quotient identities; claim-log-laws and its same-scene notes continue to resolve to that record.

Two scoped inference claims now record the discrete-factor/continuous-rate equivalence and exact-model half-life relation, their limitations, the existing DLMF support, and every scene where those relationships are taught or applied; each mapped scene exposes the claims in its source note.

objective-interpret-change now promises calculation and interpretation of threshold times and half-lives from discrete or continuous decay models, matching item-half-life and item-transfer-decay rather than claiming unassessed doubling-time or growth-factor mastery.
item-solve-exponential now has the literal acceptable response 4.865, consistent with ln(7)/0.4 = 4.864775... rounded to the requested three decimal places and with its rubric.

Independent recalculation again gives 6.8841987059... and confirms the candidate's bracket, substitution, and continuous-versus-completed-interval interpretation, so the accurate worked example was retained.
The starting diagnostic is now a semantic check directive with concrete answers and explanations plus the existing non-punitive exponent-law recovery route.
Each of the four practice prompts is now a separate check directive, all prompts precede a later feedback section, and the feedback explains the governing relation or next check.

The transfer scene now contains only the independent check prompt, required reasoning components, boundary, and source note; its worked solution begins the next synthesis scene after the learner records a complete attempt.

The synthesis retrieval check now supplies correct answers and brief reasoning for inverse translation, positivity, the exponent-law basis of the product law, and the relation between a and k after the prompt set.
The misconception scene now elicits a prediction before its counterexample and rechecks the repaired model on the new 2 + 8 case with explanatory feedback.
The descriptive acceptable-response sentence was replaced by the literal response string 4.865; the answer and rubric retain the working and substitution-check requirements.
item-half-life now leaves acceptableResponses empty; its structured correct answer and rubric continue to require the exact-power reasoning and the labelled six-hour result.
item-transfer-decay now leaves acceptableResponses empty; its open-response answer and analytic rubric retain the numerical, domain, and verification criteria.
The ratio explanation is now inside check-log-solution and explicitly labelled as feedback after attempting rather than exposed as an unstructured paragraph outside the check.
This was a confirming note rather than a requested correction; the valid lesson identity, version, scene order, objective IDs, and file mappings were preserved.

evidenceChecked: The final lesson.json scene order and every scene source: each of seven scenes has one level-one heading, a logical source order, and balanced allowed directives.

Prompt and feedback paths: prerequisite and synthesis feedback follows its prompt inside semantic checks, practice feedback follows all four prompts, and transfer feedback is deferred to the next scene; all routes are textual, untimed, keyboard-compatible, and require no drag, hover, color, sound, motion, or pointer precision.

Mathematical access and reflow source: equations use dollar-delimited TeX, important relations are interpreted in prose, and the Markdown contains no raw HTML, entities, fixed layout, images, remote embeds, or position-only instructions.

claims.json, references.json, and every same-scene source note: all five claims resolve to the two existing complete NIST records, with scope, uncertainty, factual-use basis, exact locators, dated agent-access records, and no human-only source.
lesson.json thirdPartyAssets and the complete pack tree: there are no third-party assets, datasets, media, symlinks, active SVG, code, scripts, personal data, patient material, or secrets.

ATTRIBUTION.md, lesson authorship metadata, both frozen review artifacts, and this adjudication provenance: the accountable principal, author run, review runs, finalizer run, CC BY 4.0 grant, original-synthesis statement, fictional contexts, and non-clinical boundary are disclosed.
limitations: The assigned scope prohibited reading site/ schemas and running npm, so this run did not execute JSON Schema, strict source-preflight, repository, graph, or protected-branch validation; only pack-local JSON, link, field, arithmetic, Markdown, and static security checks were performed.

No rendered browser, 200% zoom/reflow, screen-reader, keyboard, print, or non-web export test was available; the accessibility conclusion is limited to the semantic source and equivalent textual paths.
This run did not reopen the NIST pages or independently recheck current source terms; it inspected the dated access and rights records and the academic review's stated independent source check.

COMMENTED GitHub transports and their non-dismissed state were supplied by the operator and were not independently queried; declared providers, models, runs, and provenance are attested, not verified independence.

rationale: The current tree differs from the exact reviewed candidate only by the two frozen review artifacts and this single finalization pass. The standard-lesson input gate is satisfied on the two eligible declared advisory artifacts: one academic and one learning-design input against the same candidate, with distinct run IDs and declared providers; both requested changes, so there are zero approvals and two change requests. All 15 indexed findings are disposed, the 13 requested changes are incorporated coherently, and the two confirming notes require no content change. Pack-local cross-links, response typing, reference constraints, directive balance, raw-markup security scans, and independent arithmetic recomputation pass, and the final accessibility-and-rights source audit is satisfied subject to its stated limitations. The learning-design review's declared principal-level conflict, lack of real learner testing, attested rather than verified provenance, unavailable repository validation, and pending final commit remain visible rather than being treated as proof of independence or effectiveness. On that evidence, the final content supports merge once the listed operator-only commit, validation, and publication-transition conditions are completed.
dissent: Both eligible advisory reviewers returned request-changes against the original candidate; their 13 requested changes were incorporated, while their two confirming notes were retained as no-change-required dispositions.

The learning-design reviewer declared that github:VSBDev operated authoring, review, and finalization runs, so principal-level independence is absent even though the founding-stage policy permits one accountable operator and the declared agent runs and providers are distinct.
No real learner testing was performed, so this adjudication makes no claim of measured learning effectiveness.
conditions: The operator must commit this one-pass final content with both cited review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character SHA, and make no further teaching-content change after the recorded commit.

Before merge, the operator must run the required strict source preflight and repository/schema validation and obtain the protected pull-request checks; this scoped run was expressly prohibited from running npm or reading site/.

The maintainer must make only the permitted post-finalization publication transition for lesson status/source confidence and retained claim review-status fields, without representing the advisory inputs as verified independence.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable fresh finalizer and adjudicator
